notes: CORBA-based, object-oriented high-end dynamic web application platform; supported personalized content, CRM, knowledge management, e-commerce. Still exists as BroadVision Commerce Agility Suite in evolved form.
Low-end dynamic web server technology; Aberdeen notes poor scaling with large user loads and high database overhead.
BroadVision One-to-One is CORBA-compliant; enables scaling and integration with IBM and Oracle CORBA systems.
Aberdeen predicted smart cards would bridge internet and physical retail; chip cards now universal (EMV standard) but used differently than Aberdeen envisioned.
PointCast and similar 'push' tech; Aberdeen dismissed as 'automated pull' not true personalization; category essentially disappeared by 2000.
Aberdeen's three-tier category (CGI/ODBC/CORBA) accurately described market structure; category evolved into modern web application frameworks (Django, Ruby on Rails, Node.js, etc.).
Listed as BroadVision One-to-One supported database; also Sybase 10x/11x, Informix 7x, MS SQL Server 6x.
BroadVision combines OO middleware with TP monitor technology to handle intense internet loads; key architectural differentiation.
